question_text: A Kalimati wholesale vegetable distributor offers you a 15% discount if you pay Rs. 30,000 cash 10 days in advance, but your digital wallet reserves are tight. What do you do?
options.A.text: Politely decline. Keeping emergency cash reserves in the wallet is critical to protect daily tea shop cash flow.
options.B.text: Secure the discount! Spend the wallet reserves and count on high vegetable sales in the next few days to replenish.
options.C.text: Reach out to a trusted peer merchant in your bazaar to borrow the cash, securing the discount without emptying your reserves.
options.D.text: Avoid advances entirely and pay higher price on delivery.

question_text: A customer who runs a local mobile repair shop asks for Rs. 5,000 worth of goods on informal bazaar credit (Udharo) without signing a formal ledger. How do you respond?
options.A.text: Decline informal request. Insist on recording the credit terms digitally in an app or physical ledger before handing over goods.
options.B.text: Provide it immediately. They are a close neighbor, and community networks require trusting their word with zero paperwork.
options.C.text: Agree to a smaller credit amount (Rs. 2,000 max) as a test of their creditworthiness, and set a strict repayment deadline.
options.D.text: Report them to bazaar committee for bad credit habits.

question_text: Your monthly NEA electricity bill is issued, but you notice a minor error that will require visiting the local office to resolve. What is your action?
options.A.text: Pay the bill immediately via eSewa to preserve a clean on-time payment track, and resolve the dispute later.
options.B.text: Delay the payment until you visit the electricity office in person next week, even if it goes past the 5-day prompt reward window.
options.C.text: Ignore the bill entirely until they threaten connection cutoff, and focus cash flows on buying store stock.
options.D.text: Pay the bill and ignore the error to avoid bureaucratic hassle.

question_text: You intend to buy a new commercial refrigerator for your snack store. How do you plan the funding?
options.A.text: Accumulate incremental digital savings over 6 months in your wallet, showing steady growth before purchasing.
options.B.text: Take a high-interest local informal loan (Sahakari or local lender) to buy it today, hoping it increases sales instantly.
options.C.text: Apply for a digital, dynamic micro-credit loan using your verified transaction history, choosing a plan matched to your weekly volume.
options.D.text: Buy a cheap second-hand refrigerator with cash flow without thinking of warranty or efficiency.
